sn35.101:1.1: “Monks, whatever’s not yours: Let go of it.
sn35.101:1.2: Your letting go of it will be for your long-term happiness & benefit.
sn35.101:1.6: Your letting go of it will be for your long-term happiness & benefit.
sn35.101:1.16: Whatever arises in dependence on eye-contact—experienced either as pleasure, as pain, or as neither-pleasure-nor-pain—that too isn’t yours:
sn35.101:1.18: Your letting go of it will be for your long-term happiness & benefit.
sn35.101:3.3: Your letting go of it will be for your long-term happiness & benefit.
sn35.101:3.13: Whatever arises in dependence on intellect-contact—experienced either as pleasure, as pain, or as neither-pleasure-nor-pain—that too isn’t yours:
sn35.101:3.15: Your letting go of it will be for your long-term happiness & benefit.
sn35.101:4.1: “Suppose a person were to gather or burn or do as he likes with the grass, twigs, branches, & leaves here in Jeta’s Grove.
sn35.101:4.2: Would the thought occur to you,
sn35.101:4.3: ‘It’s us that this person is gathering, burning, or doing with as he likes’?”
sn35.101:7.1: Because those things are not our self nor do they pertain to our self.”
sn35.101:8.1: “In the same way, monks, the eye isn’t yours:
sn35.101:8.3: Your letting go of it will be for your long-term happiness & benefit…
sn35.101:8.7: Whatever arises in dependence on intellect-contact—experienced either as pleasure, as pain, or as neither-pleasure-nor-pain—that too isn’t yours:
sn35.101:8.9: Your letting go of it will be for your long-term happiness & benefit.”
